source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Presumptive Modes of Appearance - Family Matters
text: Presumptive modes of appearance for family matters in the Central West Region: Early or Urgent Case Conferences: virtual; Urgent Motions: in writing; Case Conferences, Settlement Conferences and Trial Management Conferences: IN PERSON; Assignment Court / Trial Scheduling Conference: virtual; Procedural & Consent Motions: in writing; Short motions: virtual; Long Motions: IN PERSON; Trials: IN PERSON; DRO conferences: virtual. For ROTA weekly circuit courts in Guelph, Orangeville, Walkerton, and Owen Sound, subject to judicial direction, all types of events scheduled during a ROTA Day will be heard in person notwithstanding the presumptive mode. Only where a timely request and clear and compelling reasons exist will a change of presumptive mode be granted.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Requests to Change the Presumptive Mode of Hearing
text: Any party seeking to change the Presumptive Mode of Hearing for a civil or family event must request, in writing, a virtual conference to seek the change PRIOR TO THE MATTER BEING SCHEDULED OR AT THE EARLIEST OPPORTUNITY upon the hearing being scheduled. If a change in the presumptive mode is granted, the scheduled hearing date will have to be adjourned to a new date. Requests for a change in the Presumptive Mode of Hearing within two weeks of the scheduled hearing date will NOT be considered.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Filing Court Documents - Central West Region
text: Court documents which do not comply with document standards, including the use of 12 font, double spacing, margin minimums and compliance with the maximum length for any Court document, will NOT be accepted for filing. Documents MUST be filed through the appropriate portal or they will be rejected. The Court will ONLY accept filings by e-mail or in-person for: (1) matters that are urgent, including requests for an urgent hearing; (2) documents that must be filed for a Court date that is less than 3 business days away; (3) documents that must be filed for a deadline that is less than 3 business days away; or (4) where alternate filing requirements are required due to an accessibility request. Family court documents shall be electronically filed through Family Submissions Online (www.ontario.ca/familyclaims).

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Email Addresses for Filing - Central West Region
text: For urgent/limited email filing of civil and family matters: Brampton (Peel Region Courthouse): BramptonSCJCourt@ontario.ca; Milton (Halton Region Courthouse): MiltonSCJCourt@ontario.ca; Orangeville (Dufferin County Courthouse): OrangevilleSCJCourt@ontario.ca; Guelph (Wellington County Courthouse): SCJtrialofficeguelph@ontario.ca; Owen Sound (Grey County Courthouse): OwenSound.SCJ.Courts@ontario.ca; Walkerton (Bruce County Courthouse): Walkerton.SCJ.Courts@ontario.ca.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Email Addresses for Confirmation Forms - Central West Region
text: Confirmation forms for civil motions, family motions, and family conferences must be filed electronically to: Brampton (Peel Region Courthouse): scjtrialofficebrampton@ontario.ca; Milton (Halton Region Courthouse): SCJHaltontrialoffice@ontario.ca; Orangeville (Dufferin County Courthouse): OrangevilleSCJTrialOffice@ontario.ca; Guelph (Wellington County Courthouse): SCJtrialofficeguelph@ontario.ca; Owen Sound (Grey County Courthouse): SCJGreyBruce@ontario.ca; Walkerton (Bruce County Courthouse): SCJGreyBruce@ontario.ca.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Uploading Documents (Case Center) - Central West Region
text: All scheduled civil and family events will use Case Center except for Assignment Court and Triage Court. Upon service and filing of documents, counsel must immediately upload the filed documents to Case Center. In addition to the materials listed in the Provincial Practice Direction that should not be uploaded, counsel and parties in the Central West Region are also not to upload: (1) Children's Aid Societies (CAS) records; (2) Financial records where confidential identifying information has not been redacted; (3) Other private confidential documentation such as children's health records; and (4) Affidavits for motions to get off the record unless privileged information has been redacted.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Triage Court - Brampton (Tuesdays) and Milton (Thursdays)
text: In Brampton and Milton, the following matters may be dealt with at Triage Court: consent matters; requests for setting a peremptory timetable for long motions; and where long motions are ready for a hearing, schedule a long motion hearing date. Brampton Triage Court is held virtually on Tuesday mornings at 9:00 am. Milton Triage Court is held virtually on Thursday mornings at 9:00 am. Each matter is limited to 10 minutes or less. Gowns are not required. To be placed on the Triage Court list, counsel or parties must file a Triage Court Requisition Form with the Trial Coordinators' Office at least two business days before the Triage Court date. For Brampton: scjtrialofficebrampton@ontario.ca; For Milton: SCJHaltontrialoffice@ontario.ca.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Obtaining a Case Conference Date in Brampton
text: Counsel and parties must go to Calendly (https://calendly.com/brampton-scj) to schedule their Conference. The Trial Coordinator's Office will not schedule conferences by phone or by email. Calendly will not accept scheduling a Conference within 10 days of the hearing date. If a Conference is re-scheduled, it is the responsibility of counsel or the parties to ensure the previously scheduled date is vacated in Calendly. Cancellations or adjournments using Calendly must be on consent. Cancellations and adjournments will not be accepted on Calendly within 10 days of the scheduled hearing date. Adjournment requests not on consent or within 10 days must be obtained from the RSJ or LAJ prior to the Conference. If a further date is obtained in Court during the hearing, counsel/parties must immediately reserve that date in Calendly. Failure to do so may result in a further adjournment. Brampton no longer schedules Early Case Conferences.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Obtaining a Case Conference Date - Milton, Guelph, Orangeville, Walkerton, Owen Sound
text: Conferences should be scheduled by emailing the Court requesting available dates at: Milton: SCJHaltontrialoffice@ontario.ca; Orangeville: OrangevilleSCJTrialOffice@ontario.ca; Guelph: SCJtrialofficeguelph@ontario.ca; Owen Sound: SCJGreyBruce@ontario.ca; Walkerton: SCJGreyBruce@ontario.ca. Late filing: Neither Court staff nor Judicial Assistants can or will accept late Conference briefs or briefs which do not comply with restrictions. Requests to file late must be made to the presiding judge and may result in an adjournment and/or cost consequences.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Case Conference Briefs and Requirements - All Central West
text: Case Conference Briefs must not exceed 8 pages, plus permissible attachments and additional documents required by the Family Law Rules. This 8-page limit includes the brief itself (Form 17A) and any additional pages attached as an appendix or schedule. If disclosure is not resolved prior to the Case Conference, any party seeking disclosure must include a detailed list of outstanding disclosure attached to the brief. The Case Conference Brief should expressly set out any orders that a party seeks. The Case Conference judge has jurisdiction to make substantive orders in appropriate cases (FLR 17(8)(b.1)). Prior to attending, parties must confer and discuss resolution; failure may result in adjournment. Confirmation Forms are to be filed and uploaded to Case Center by 2 pm three days prior to the hearing, or the Case Conference is cancelled.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Proceeding to Settlement Conference After Case Conference
text: If resolution was not achieved at the Case Conference, the primary aim will be to ensure a useful Settlement Conference. The presiding judge will order a timetable for completion of any outstanding disclosure, order questioning, identify motions to be brought, and identify whether expert evidence is necessary. Settlement Conferences will be scheduled well into the future to permit motions, disclosure, or other steps to be completed. On an appropriate matter (e.g. Rule 15 Motion to Change), the Case Conference judge may timetable the matter directly to a long motion date or a Trial Scheduling Conference. Once a Case or Settlement Conference has been scheduled, no adjournments will be permitted except once on consent of the parties 10 days before the conference date or with a Court order.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Scheduling Settlement Conferences in Brampton
text: Settlement Conferences can only be scheduled by a Dispute Resolution Officer (DRO) or a Superior Court Justice upon being satisfied that the parties are ready. Settlement Conferences will generally be heard only on Mondays. These dates can be obtained: At a DRO Conference; At a Procedural Case Conference; or On a 14B Motion.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Settlement Conference Requirements - All Central West
text: Settlement Conference requirements: (1) Confirmations must be emailed to the Court no later than 2 pm, 3 business days before the date; Confirmation Forms must also be uploaded to Case Center; (2) A Settlement Conference Brief not to exceed 12 pages, plus permissible attachments; (3) If financial issues, an up-to-date Financial Statement and Certificate of Financial Disclosure; (4) If parenting is at issue, an updated Form 35.1 Parenting Affidavit with assessments/reports attached; (5) If child or spousal support is at issue, Divorce Mate calculations for each year in dispute and a chart calculating proposed net arrears; (6) If equalization is at issue, a comparative Net Family Property with a Brief of contested values; (7) A Rule 18 Offer to Settle; (8) A draft Order. All litigants must be present at the Settlement Conference. No party may schedule a second Settlement Conference without approval of a judge.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Family Assignment Court and TMC in Brampton
text: At the conclusion of the Settlement Conference, the judge will provide the parties with a Family Assignment Court date. At Family Assignment Court, upon being satisfied that there are no outstanding issues and parties are ready for trial, the presiding judge will schedule a TMC and a trial date. If the matter is not ready for trial, the presiding judge will make an order for directions.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Trial Management Conference Requirements - All Central West
text: Adjournments of TMCs will rarely be granted. Even with consent, TMCs may only be adjourned with a judge's approval. Requests must be made to the RSJ or RSJ designate and must set out compelling reasons. Any adjournment will result in a significantly delayed trial date. Parties must file and upload: a joint proposed Trial Scheduling Endorsement Form (TSEF); updated Certificates of Financial Disclosure; Draft Orders, Opening Statements; and Offers to Settle. If a party refuses to cooperate on a joint TSEF, each party shall prepare a separate one, and cost consequences may follow. The TMC judge will ensure counsel provide reasonable time estimates for the entire trial. Subject to the trial judge's discretion, the endorsement and TSEF will govern the conduct of the trial. Breach of orders at the TMC may result in striking pleadings, exclusion of evidence, contempt, or costs.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Form 14B Motions and Basket Motions
text: Simple, procedural, consent and known uncontested matters must be brought by a 14B Motion. A draft Order in WORD format must be filed. In Brampton, consent motions may be brought before the Triage Court. Contested motions should not be filed as basket motions; they should be scheduled for a regular or short motions Court. Rule 14B Motions should rarely be used for making substantive orders (such as parenting, decision-making) except in the clearest urgency or on consent. Uploading to Case Center is NOT required. For uncontested trials: if no Answer has been filed, an Applicant may seek final Orders on an uncontested trial by 14B Motion (Rule 23(22)). A draft Form 25D Order must be filed, and all trial materials must be uploaded to Case Center.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Urgent Motions with or without Notice
text: Urgent motions meet the requirements of Rules 14(4.2) and 14(12)-(15) of the Family Law Rules. A party may seek an urgent motion in advance of a Case Conference only in situations of urgency or hardship, including: immediate danger of a child's removal from Ontario, immediate danger to health or safety of a child or the moving party, and threats of harm. Any motion filed as urgent will first be reviewed by a judge to determine whether it is urgent. If urgent, the judge will provide directions on when it will be heard; in Brampton and Milton, the judge may refer the matter to Triage Court. A party seeking a motion without notice must also set out why notice is unnecessary or not reasonably possible. A factum or Summary of Argument is not required for an urgent motion without notice. If the judge determines the motion is not urgent, the party must schedule it in the normal fashion, and costs consequences may apply.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Scheduling Short/Regular Motions in Brampton
text: Short/Regular motions are motions which will take less than one hour in total. Filing a cross-motion does NOT extend the estimated time. Counsel and parties must go to Calendly (https://calendly.com/brampton-scj) to schedule Short/Regular Motions unless it is within 10 days of the hearing date. Cancellations or adjournments must only be done on consent. Neither cancellations nor adjournments can be processed in Calendly more than 10 days prior to the hearing date. If opposing counsel has not cooperated on selecting a date, the moving party may unilaterally schedule through Calendly (keep records of attempts for costs purposes).

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Short Motion Materials and Page Limits
text: Except for motions arising from the wrongful removal or retention of a child, each party is restricted to one primary affidavit which shall not exceed 12 pages of narrative. Exhibits shall be limited to only the necessary and relevant evidence and are generally expected not to exceed 10 pages. Leave is required to exceed these restrictions and will only be granted in exceptional circumstances. Court documents which do not comply will NOT be accepted for filing. Late filing will not be accepted by Court staff; the request must be addressed by the motions judge. Judicial Assistants have no authority to accept ANY Court filing; counsel must not deliver materials to Judicial Assistants unless expressly directed by a judge.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Confirmation Forms for Short Motions
text: Unless dispensed by Court order, the moving party must email a Form 14C: Confirmation Form no later than 2 p.m. three business days before the date of the motion. Parties may file a Confirmation Form jointly. This applies even if the matter has been directed to be heard peremptorily. Where Confirmation Forms have not been filed by at least one party, the hearing date will be vacated. The Confirmation Form must list the specific issues to be addressed and clearly set out which materials are necessary for the judge to review. It must include an appropriate time estimate for the entire motion. Confirmation forms must be uploaded to Case Center into the correct bundle.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Long Motions in Brampton and Milton
text: A complete Motion Record is to be served by the moving party with a return date of TBA. Parties must discuss and agree on a timetable in writing for all necessary steps. If parties cannot agree within 45 days of service of the Moving Party Record, any party can request an attendance at Triage Court. Once the timetable is complied with, any party can request a hearing date at Triage Court. A factum (or Summary of Argument) is required on all long motions; if the moving party does not file one, the motion date will be cancelled. Facta are limited to 20 pages. All long motions must be confirmed by filing Confirmation Forms no later than 2pm, 7 days prior to the hearing date. If on consent, adjournments require at least 7 days' notice to the TCO. Adjournments within 7 days will only be granted for clear and compelling reasons. If adjourned, parties must re-attend Triage Court for a new date with NO priority.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Long Motions in Orangeville, Guelph, Walkerton, and Owen Sound
text: Motions expected to take an hour or longer must be scheduled as long motions through the Trial Coordinator's Office. To obtain a date: confer with opposing party on possible dates and estimated time, then obtain a date from the TCO. After obtaining the date, the moving party must serve and file motion materials within 10 days (Notice of Motion, supporting affidavits, draft Order, Proof of Service); failure may result in the date being vacated. Parties must agree on a timetable within 30 days or request a procedural conference. The moving party's factum must be served and filed three weeks prior to the hearing date; the responding party's factum two weeks prior. Consent adjournments must be more than 21 days prior. Only one consent adjournment will be granted. Opposed adjournments more than 21 days prior require a virtual attendance before a judge. Within 21 days, adjournments must be sought from the motions judge on the hearing date.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: DRO Program - Brampton
text: Counsel and parties must go to Calendly (https://calendly.com/brampton-scj) to schedule their DRO Conference. DRO Conferences will not and cannot be scheduled by the Trial Coordinator's Office by email or by telephone. Counsel and parties are required to canvass agreeable dates, but if a date cannot be agreed upon, either party can unilaterally select a date, bearing in mind the notice requirements in the Family Law Rules. Cancellations or adjournments must only be done on consent. Neither cancellations nor adjournments can be processed in Calendly more than 10 days prior to the scheduled hearing date.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: DRO Program - Guelph, Orangeville, Walkerton, Owen Sound
text: DRO conferences in Guelph, Walkerton, Owen Sound and Orangeville are available for all family matters. As DRO conferences are conducted virtually, the DROs scheduled in Brampton can conduct DRO conferences in these four locations via Zoom. Counsel and parties that wish to schedule a DRO conference on a Guelph, Walkerton, Owen Sound or Orangeville family case can do so by booking through the Brampton Calendly site: https://calendly.com/brampton-scj. Filing of material for the DRO conference, including a confirmation form, will be at the court location site as usual.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: DRO Program - Milton
text: DRO Conferences should be scheduled by emailing the Court requesting available dates at Milton – Halton Region Courthouse: SCJHaltontrialoffice@ontario.ca.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Motions to Change - Central West
text: The Court must determine the next steps in a Motion to Change and the most appropriate process for reaching an expeditious and just conclusion. Rule 15 Motions to Change seek variations or reviews of SCJ parenting and support Orders. It is important to clearly identify whether the Order(s) sought are pursuant to Section 17 of the Divorce Act or Section 37 of the Family Law Act. Parties must clearly identify whether the change is a review or a variation and, if a variation, set out the material change(s) in circumstances. Parties to a Motion to Change in Brampton and Milton must first attend a Case Conference with a DRO, unless there is an Order allowing them to waive that Conference. The DRO will confirm MIP attendance, mediation consideration, and Rule 18 Offers to Settle before moving forward. Family Law Rule 15(26) permits the Court to determine the Motion to Change on a final basis on the material filed unless the judge determines that a trial is needed.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Trial Record
text: The Applicant or the Respondent (if a claim is advanced by the Respondent) must file a Trial Record at least 20 days prior to the scheduled Trial date. Failure to do so may result in the matter being removed from the Trial list unless the Court orders otherwise. Trial dates, when scheduled, will rarely be adjourned even on consent. Any adjournment requests, even when on consent, must be made by way of a motion which will be heard by the RSJ or the LAJ or the RSJ designate.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Cost Orders in Family Law Motions and Applications
text: Pursuant to Rule 24(10) of the Family Law Rules, costs should be determined in a summary manner promptly after dealing with each step in a case. Any claim for costs shall be supported by satisfactory documentation. Any party opposing a claim for costs shall provide documentation showing their own fees and expenses (Rule 24(12.2)). The parties should attempt to resolve the quantum of costs prior to the hearing. If unable to resolve, all counsel appearing on motions and conferences must attend with their Costs Outline. If the Costs Outline is not available, the judge may decline to make a costs award in their favour.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Direct to a Combined Case/Settlement Conference
text: To encourage litigants to resolve disputes through family dispute resolution, requests can be made to move directly to a combined case conference/settlement conference where the parties have tried to resolve disputed issues through mediation or a Legal Aid Ontario settlement conference. These requests can only be made where: (1) there are no outstanding temporary issues; and (2) neither party is seeking additional disclosure. These requests are made by filing a 14B Motion Form along with Form 17G: Certificate of Dispute Resolution. If granted, the parties must file all required Settlement Conference materials.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Motions in Writing - Family
text: Where all interested parties agree that a motion may be decided by written materials and submissions (without an attendance in person or virtually), they must agree on a timetable and when all Court documents are ready, file same with the Court. Uploading of materials into Case Center is NOT required.

source: Consolidated Practice Direction for the Central West Region
heading: Automatic Orders - Central West
text: An automatic order will be issued when certain claims are made in an Application, Motion to Change or Answer in accordance with Rule 8.0.1 of the Family Law Rules. The party who commenced the claim will receive a copy and MUST serve it on the opposing parties. An Affidavit of Service should be filed and uploaded to Case Center. Rule 13(3.1) sets out which documents MUST be included with a Financial Statement where child or spousal support has been sought. A party who has not made all reasonable efforts to comply with their disclosure obligations may face cost sanctions, directions/terms, and adjournment. In egregious cases, continued non-compliance may result in pleadings being struck or a finding of contempt.
